summary: Audit customer-core screens against their PDF design specs; report exact deviations

result.summary: The reel feed structure (creator row, caption, right-rail actions, tagged product card) is all present and top-to-bottom order is correct, but several elements deviate hard from spec. Biggest issues: the Follow button uses a green fill instead of the spec's WHITE solid fill with #52525C text; the right-rail action buttons are individual semi-opaque black circles instead of the single glassmorphic rounded-pill container (radius 20, rgba(51,51,51,0.60), blur 20, padding 16) the PDF shows; the tagged-product "Add to Cart" is rendered as a green filled button labeled "Add" instead of an underlined green TEXT LINK reading "Add to Cart"; and the product card / thumbnail dimensions, radii, background and price styling are all off. Caption typography (size/line-height) and action count typography are also wrong. Empty-state and error copy are app-authored (no spec equivalent) and left as-is.

result.summary: The code captures the basic structure (header "Comments", scrollable comment list, sticky composer) but diverges heavily from the spec. Major omissions: the entire per-comment like affordance (like icon + likes count column) is absent, the @handle username styling is wrong (code shows display name at 14px/600 instead of @handle at 10px/600 muted), the dot separator between handle and time is missing, and nearly every typography token (sizes, weights, colors, line-heights) is off. The composer differs in avatar (missing), shape (pill radius 999 vs 8px), placeholder ellipsis character, fill/border, and padding. The screen is also presented as a full Scaffold rather than the spec's rounded (28px) bottom sheet.

deviations.spec: Each comment row has a trailing like column: heart Like Icon 20x20, fill #9F9FA9 (Icon-Light), liked state #FB2C36 (Icon-Error-Default), with Likes Count below it 10px/600 Poppins muted #9F9FA9 center (e.g. 234, 2.1k, 456). Tappable like toggle.

deviations.fix: Add a trailing Column at end of the comment Row: an IconButton/GestureDetector with a 20x20 heart icon (Icons.favorite_border / Icons.favorite) colored DesignTokens.iconLight (#9F9FA9) or #FB2C36 when liked, and a Text below it for the like count styled 10px/w600/Poppins/#9F9FA9 centered.

result.summary: The screen's structure and section order (Title → Search field → Popular Searches → Browse by Category → Trending Now → Top Creators) match the spec, and the search field copy/placeholder are exact. But there are several real deviations: the Section Titles use the wrong typography token (18px/600/white instead of the spec's 14px/400/#D4D4D8), the pills use the wrong fill color, text weight, text color, padding, and radius, and the screen header uses a tune/filter icon that the spec does not document. The "Trending Now" header emoji and the "Discover more creators" CTA also deviate from the spec wording. Copy on static labels is otherwise close, but the pill and section-title styling and the undocumented header icon are the load-bearing mismatches.

result.summary: The PDF specifies a standalone full-screen "Customer Reviews" page: a screen header titled "Customer Reviews" with a back icon, two tabs ("Reel Reviews" / "Written Reviews") with an active-tab green underline, a grid of video-review thumbnails (107x133, 12px radius) each with a blurred view-count pill (eye icon + compact count like 12.3m), and a sticky bottom "Add Review" button. None of this exists. The audited Dart file is product_detail_screen.dart, which only renders a tiny inline "Reviews (n)" section with a "See All" link (non-functional onTap) and a "No reviews yet" empty state — it does not navigate to or implement the spec'd Customer Reviews screen, and no such screen file exists anywhere in the discovery feature. This is a major gap: the entire spec'd screen is unbuilt. Everything below is reported against the spec; nearly all are missing-element findings. Note the inline preview's own copy ("Reviews (n)", "See All", "No reviews yet") is NOT in the spec and cannot be validated against it — but the dead "See All" onTap should route to the (to-be-built) Customer Reviews screen.

deviations.fix: Create a new CustomerReviewsScreen in lib/features/customer/discovery/presentation/screens/ implementing the spec, and route the product detail 'See All' tap to it

result.summary: The cart screen is missing nearly the entire lower half of the spec: the spec defines a full "Bill Details" breakdown card (Sub Total, Shipping, Tax, Grand Total), the "You are appreciated" creator-thank-you card, a "Have a Promo Code?" promo button, and a two-line "Total Order" sticky checkout bar — none of which exist in code. The implemented sticky bar uses a single total + item count instead of the spec's "Total Order" label/amount layout, and the button label is "Proceed to Checkout" vs spec "Proceed to checkout". Per-item rows omit the "From: @handle (XX% Commission)" creator-affiliate line and the "Save for later" action. Screen title is "My Cart" vs spec "Your Cart". Many copy and styling values deviate. Treated as major because multiple documented sections are entirely absent.

result.summary: The Dart screen implements a generic "filled" checkout (saved address card, payment card, order-items list, simple price summary, "Place Order" CTA). The PDF specifies the EMPTY-state checkout with a different element set and order: Shipping Address empty-prompt block, a styled "Bill Details" card with a "You are appreciated" sub-card, a Payment Method option LIST (Visa Card / Paypal / eSewa / Cash on Delivery), and a primary CTA labeled "Add Shipping Address" (NOT "Place Order"). Multiple required sections and the entire copy set are missing or wrong. This needs a layout rebuild, not minor tweaks.

deviations.spec: Section shows a card/row with title "Shipping Address", description "You're almost there! Add a shipping address to continue." (Description: Poppins 11px, weight 400, line-height 120%/13.2px, color Text-Light #D4D4D8), a Delivery/Truck icon (32x32, fill Icon-Secondary #F1C40F, on Fill-Secondary-Dark #3A2F03 rounded 8px / padding 4px), and a trailing Plus icon (20x20, fill Icon-Light #9F9FA9). result.summary: The implementation is a generic payment-method list (card / eSewa / COD with "Add New" buttons) that does not follow the spec's structure. The spec defines a screen titled "Payment Methods (4)" with three documented sections in order: (1) a "Secure & Encrypted" info banner, (2) a "Cards" section with an "Add Card" action and per-card list items showing brand/name/expiry/last-four plus status tags (Default / Expiring), and (3) a "Digital Wallets" section listing Paypal, Google Pay, Apple Pay with link status and a "Link" button. Major deviations: wrong screen title, the entire Secure banner is missing, the Cards/Digital Wallets section structure is missing, the card-tile copy/status-tag elements are missing, and the "Add New / Add New Card / Add eSewa / Cash on Delivery" buttons are not in the spec at all (extra). Card list items use radio buttons and last-four masking instead of the spec's "ending in" copy and More-Actions icon button.

result.summary: The PDF spec documents a "Track Order" screen whose core content is an Order Details summary card plus a 4-stage Tracking Timeline (Shipped / In Transit / Out for Delivery / Delivered) with a "View Other Details" expander. The Dart implementation is a different, generic order screen: it shows price breakdown, shipping/payment, and Cancel/Return action buttons — none of which the spec shows — and entirely omits the Tracking Timeline (the spec's central section #3) and the "View Other Details" action (#4). Screen title, header copy, label wording, date/amount layout, the info tag, and tracking-ID styling all diverge. Treat this as a major rewrite to match the spec.

result.summary: The Dart screen implements the cancel flow but diverges substantially from the Step-2 spec. The spec is a single card-wrapped form containing five labeled subsections (3.1 Order Details with a status tag + "View Order Summary" link, 3.2 Important notice with title "Important" and two specific bullets, 3.3 reason radios, 3.4 Comments textarea, 3.5 Refund Info block with Method/Amount/Time rows) and a sticky "Proceed" button. The code instead renders a flat (non-card) layout, omits the Order Details header/status-tag/View-Order-Summary link entirely, omits the Refund Info (Method/Amount/Time) block, omits the step progress bar, renames the Important notice and its bullets, replaces the spec's "Proceed" button with a red "Confirm Cancellation" button, adds an extra acknowledgement checkbox not in the spec, and rewords nearly every static label and reason option. Many copy strings differ in wording, casing, and punctuation.

result.summary: The implementation diverges fundamentally from the spec in layout and content. The spec defines a single-column vertical LIST of rich product rows (64px thumbnail + product name + variant info + price/old-price + a colored stock tag + perks/benefits + price-drop/notify link actions + a per-row "Add to Cart" button), followed by a "Pro Tip" card and a sticky bottom bar with "Add All to Cart" and "Clear All Saved Items" buttons. The code instead renders a 2-column GRID of minimal cards (image + name + green price + optional star rating) with a heart-remove icon, no stock tags, no variant line, no old price, no perks, no per-row Add to Cart, no Pro Tip card, no sticky bottom action bar, and no Filter/Sort By/stock filter chips. Most spec sections are entirely missing. The screen title matches; nearly everything else needs to be rebuilt to spec.

result.summary: The screen diverges substantially from the spec. result.summary: The spec and the implemented screen are structurally different. The spec ("Customer Reviews") is a tabbed gallery: a header titled "Customer Reviews", a two-tab switcher ("Reel Reviews" / "Written Reviews"), a grid of video-review thumbnails (107x133, 12px radius) each with a blurred views pill (icon + count), and a sticky bottom pill "Add Review" button. The code instead renders a title "Reviews", a star-rating summary card with a 1-5 distribution histogram, a vertical list of written ReviewCards, a "Load More" text button, and a floating "Write a Review" FAB that opens a write-review bottom sheet. Almost every documented element (tabs, video grid, views pills, sticky button, header copy) is missing or wrong; many code elements (summary histogram, FAB, load-more) are not in the spec. Copy mismatches on the header and the primary button are the highest-priority fixes.

result.summary: The Profile screen largely matches the spec's structure (header with avatar/name/email, 3-stat quick-stats row, grouped settings list, version footer), and copy on most labels is exact. However there are real deviations: the spec's "Push Notifications" menu row is MISSING from the code, the code ADDS a "Delete Account" row the spec does not show, and the spec's flat single settings container is split into multiple grouped sections. The first settings group's row order also diverges from the spec (spec: Shipping Addresses, Push Notifications, Payment Methods, Appearance, Notification Settings, Language). Stat labels, order, and badge match. Header copy is data-driven and consistent with spec sample values.

result.summary: The card layout is heavily simplified versus the spec. The spec shows a rich Following Creator Card with avatar, creator name, a content-category line (e.g. "Travel & Skincare"), handle, a star-rating element, and a follower count rendered as separate rating + follower groups with icons. The code collapses all of this into name + a single "@handle · N followers" line and omits the content category and star rating entirely. The screen title also omits the count "(230)", the Unfollow button styling/color is wrong (spec is a gray solid pill #3F3F46 white text; code is a borderless red text button), avatar size is wrong (40px spec vs 48px code), and the empty-state copy doesn't match any spec string but the spec implies different content. Title count, content category section, and star rating are the highest-impact misses.
